children.heading: Assessment and strategies for forest resources
children.label: a Assessment and strategies for forest resources
A
children.text: the conditions and trends of forest resources in that State;

children.label: B
children.text: the threats to forest lands and resources in that State consistent with the national priorities specified in ;section 2101(c) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: any areas or regions of that State that are a priority; and

children.label: D
children.text: any multi-State areas that are a regional priority.

children.label: A
children.text: strategies for addressing threats to forest resources in the State outlined in the assessment required by paragraph (1); and

children.label: B
children.text: a description of the resources necessary for the State forester or equivalent State official from all sources to address the State-wide strategy.

children.heading: Updating
children.label: b Updating
children.text: At such times as the Secretary determines to be necessary, the State forester or equivalent State official shall update and resubmit to the Secretary the State-wide assessment and State-wide strategy required by subsection (a).
the State Forest Stewardship Coordinating Committee established for the State under ;section 2113(b) of this title
the State wildlife agency, with respect to strategies contained in the State wildlife action plans;

as feasible, appropriate military installations where the voluntary participation and management of private or State-owned or other public forestland is able to support, promote, and contribute to the missions of such installations; and
for purposes of the Forest Legacy Program under , the State lead agency designated by the Governor.section 2103c of this title

children.heading: Incorporation of other plans
children.label: d Incorporation of other plans
children.text: In developing or updating the State-wide assessment and State-wide strategy required by subsection (a), the State forester or equivalent State official shall incorporate any forest management plan of the State, including community wildfire protection plans and State wildlife action plans.

children.heading: Sufficiency
children.label: e Sufficiency
children.text: Once approved by the Secretary, a State-wide assessment and State-wide strategy developed under subsection (a) shall be deemed to be sufficient to satisfy all relevant State planning and assessment requirements under this chapter.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 1 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section up to $10,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2008 through 2023.

children.heading: Additional funding sources
children.label: 2 Additional funding sources
children.text: In addition to the funds appropriated for a fiscal year pursuant to the authorization of appropriations in paragraph (1) to carry out this section, the Secretary may use any other funds made available for planning under this chapter to carry out this section, except that the total amount of combined funding used to carry out this section may not exceed $10,000,000 in any fiscal year.

children.heading: Annual report on use of funds
children.label: g Annual report on use of funds
children.text: The State forester or equivalent State official shall submit to the Secretary an annual report detailing how funds made available to the State under this chapter are being used.
